data.title: SereneMind: design and evaluation of a persuasive mobile app for managing stress among adults
data.authors: Mona Alhasani, Oladapo Oyebode, Rita Orji
data.conference: 2023 IEEE 11th international conference on serious games and applications for health (SeGAH)
data.description: Stress is a global public health problem that leads to severe physical and psychological health risks if not managed. Various stress management interventions have been delivered using mobile health (mHealth) apps. Although there are different existing mobile apps promoting stress-coping strategies, more work is still needed to design and evaluate such apps from a persuasion standpoint. Persuasive mobile apps incorporate persuasive strategies (PS) that work as backbone mechanics to motivate desired behaviour change. Therefore, we design and evaluate a mobile app called SereneMind following a five-step process. First, we deconstruct the PS operationalized in 150 stress management apps from both Google Play and App Store to identify the most implemented PS using the Persuasive Systems Design (PSD) framework. Second, we design a low-fidelity prototype (LFP) depicting various features of the app …
